steps.title: Distribute the negative signs
steps.content: First rewrite the expression by removing parentheses, applying the minus signs: $$(9x^{4}+8x^{3})-(7x^{3}+5x^{2})-(3x^{2}+x)=9x^{4}+8x^{3}-7x^{3}-5x^{2}-3x^{2}-x.$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Now combine the coefficients of each power of $$x$$:
  • $$x^{4}$$: $$9$$ (only term).
  • $$x^{3}$$: $$8-7=1$$.
  • $$x^{2}$$: $$-5-3=-8$$.
  • $$x$$: $$-1$$.
This gives $$9x^{4}+x^{3}-8x^{2}-x$$, which matches the form $$9x^{4}+x^{3}+ax^{2}-x$$ with $$a=-8$$.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
